evals.name: scenario-a-dm2-lean-saas
evals.prompt:
I need DM 2 for a LinkedIn prospect who accepted my connection 4 days ago but hasn't replied. Profile:

Name: Sarah Chen
Role: Co-founder & CEO
Company: Growlio (SaaS, analytics for D2C brands)
Company size: 4
Bio mentions: ex-Shopify, building out of Toronto
Recent post: shared a thread about how much of her week is going to manual reporting instead of product work

Previous DM I sent (DM 1, Day 0):
"Hey Sarah, thanks for connecting! It looks like you're running Growlio pretty lean which usually means you're wearing more hats than you'd like. What's the part of the business eating the most of your time right now?"

Write DM 2 for Day 4.
evals.expected_output: A Scenario A DM 2 output: under 1000 chars, value-only (no pitch, no call ask), includes a Voss label, references her role/stage/recent post in a way that doesn't repeat DM 1's phrasing, signs off with '- Aleem' or nothing, no emojis, no em dashes, no 'just following up' or 'circling back' openers.

assertions.name: under_1000_chars
assertions.description: Message body is under 1000 characters

assertions.name: contains_voss_label
assertions.description: Contains at least one Voss label: 'It sounds like', 'It looks like', 'It seems like', or 'It feels like'

assertions.name: no_em_dashes
assertions.description: Contains no em dashes (—) — only plain hyphens allowed

assertions.name: no_emojis
assertions.description: Contains no emoji characters

assertions.name: no_following_up_phrases
assertions.description: Does not contain 'just following up', 'bumping this', 'circling back', or 'touching base'

assertions.name: no_call_ask_in_dm2
assertions.description: Does not ask for a call, meeting, Zoom, or 15/20-minute chat

assertions.name: no_pitch_in_dm2
assertions.description: Does not pitch NexusPoint services or drop a case study / result with a percentage

assertions.name: personalized_to_profile
assertions.description: References her specific context (lean team, founder-as-integration-layer, reporting pain from her post, or similar) — not generic

assertions.name: does_not_repeat_dm1
assertions.description: Does not re-use DM 1's exact phrasing like 'wearing more hats' or 'what's eating your time'

evals.name: scenario-a-dm4-agency-coo
evals.prompt:
I need DM 4 (the final one) for a LinkedIn prospect. They accepted the connection 16 days ago, I sent DM 2 on Day 4 and DM 3 on Day 9, no reply to any of them. Profile:

Name: Marcus Ellery
Role: COO
Company: Northwind Collective (20-person marketing agency, specializes in B2B SaaS client work)
Based in Austin, TX
Company size: 20

DM 3 (Day 9) referenced our lead-to-outreach pipeline that cut manual follow-up time by 80%.

Write DM 4 for Day 16.
evals.expected_output: A Scenario A DM 4 output: under 1000 chars, direct but no-oriented call ask, includes a Voss label, permission-framed ('would it be off base', 'is it a bad idea', 'would it be crazy'), specific short duration (15-20 min), graceful exit option ('if it's not the right time, no worries'), signs off with '- Aleem' or nothing. No em dashes, no emojis.

assertions.name: under_1000_chars
assertions.description: Message body is under 1000 characters

assertions.name: contains_voss_label
assertions.description: Contains at least one Voss label

assertions.name: no_oriented_ask
assertions.description: Contains a no-oriented ask phrase: 'would it be off base', 'is it a bad idea', 'would it be crazy', 'is this the worst timing', or similar

assertions.name: not_yes_oriented
assertions.description: Does not use yes-oriented framing like 'do you want to', 'can we schedule', 'are you free'

assertions.name: specific_duration
assertions.description: Specifies a short duration like '15 minutes' or '20 minutes'

assertions.name: graceful_exit_offered
assertions.description: Offers a low-friction no option ('if not, no worries', 'if it's a bad time, all good', or similar)

assertions.name: no_em_dashes
assertions.description: Contains no em dashes

assertions.name: no_emojis
assertions.description: Contains no emoji characters

assertions.name: no_following_up_phrases
assertions.description: Does not contain 'just following up', 'bumping this', 'circling back', or 'touching base'

assertions.name: last_message_framing
assertions.description: Signals this is the last message in the sequence ('last note', 'last message from me', 'don't want to camp in your inbox', or similar)

evals.name: scenario-b-opener-reply
evals.prompt:
A prospect just replied to my connection with a very short message. What should I send back?

Conversation:
[Me, Day 0]: Hey James, thanks for connecting! It looks like you're scaling Inflect past the scrappy stage and into coordination territory which is where most of the interesting problems show up. What's the biggest friction point on your plate right now?

[James, Day 1]: Happy to connect Aleem. What do you do exactly?

Profile: James Tanaka, VP Operations at Inflect (40-person fintech infra startup, Series A, NYC).
evals.expected_output: A Scenario B output with the message + meta-line (Phase / Tactic). Message should be a Qualify-phase calibrated question — not a pitch dump. Short, one-line framing of what Aleem does (no feature list, no case study), then a calibrated question that pulls James into naming his own pain. Includes Voss label. Meta-line: Phase = Qualify, Tactic names the specific Voss move.

assertions.name: under_1000_chars
assertions.description: Message body (excluding meta-line) is under 1000 characters

assertions.name: contains_voss_label
assertions.description: Contains at least one Voss label

assertions.name: contains_calibrated_question
assertions.description: Contains a calibrated question starting with 'how' or 'what' — not a yes/no question

assertions.name: includes_meta_line
assertions.description: Output includes the meta-line with 'Phase:' and 'Tactic:' fields

assertions.name: phase_is_qualify
assertions.description: Meta-line declares phase as Qualify (not Label, Proof, Pull, or Call)

assertions.name: no_case_study_drop
assertions.description: Does not drop a quantified NexusPoint result (70%, 80%, hours-to-minutes, etc.) — too early

assertions.name: no_call_ask
assertions.description: Does not ask for a call, Zoom, or meeting

assertions.name: no_em_dashes
assertions.description: Contains no em dashes

assertions.name: no_emojis
assertions.description: Contains no emoji characters

assertions.name: matches_their_length
assertions.description: Reply is short/medium, not a 4-paragraph pitch (they sent one line — Aleem should not respond with a wall of text)

evals.name: scenario-b-pain-disclosed
evals.prompt:
Prospect replied again. What do I send back?

Conversation:
[Me]: Hey Priya, thanks for connecting! It seems like scaling Lumen past the 30-person mark usually means coordination work starts quietly eating founder hours. How are you handling that right now?

[Priya]: Honestly it's rough. We're drowning in manual onboarding for both clients and new hires. Spreadsheets and Looms everywhere, nothing really connected.

Profile: Priya Sharma, Founder/CEO, Lumen (35-person B2B SaaS, HR tech, London).
evals.expected_output: A Scenario B output. This is the Label phase — she disclosed pain, Aleem should mirror it back sharper than she said it. One Voss label minimum. Optionally a short calibrated follow-up question. Should NOT drop a case study yet (save proof for when she asks 'what do you do' or confirms the label). Should NOT ask for a call. Meta-line phase should be Label. Matches her message length (she sent 2 sentences — Aleem replies with 2-4 sentences max).

assertions.name: under_1000_chars
assertions.description: Message body (excluding meta-line) is under 1000 characters

assertions.name: contains_voss_label
assertions.description: Contains at least one Voss label: 'It sounds like', 'It looks like', 'It seems like', or 'It feels like'

assertions.name: includes_meta_line
assertions.description: Output includes Phase: and Tactic: meta-line

assertions.name: phase_is_label
assertions.description: Meta-line declares phase as Label

assertions.name: no_case_study_yet
assertions.description: Does not drop a quantified NexusPoint result — she hasn't asked for proof yet, still in Label phase

assertions.name: no_call_ask
assertions.description: Does not ask for a call or meeting

assertions.name: no_pitch
assertions.description: Does not pitch NexusPoint services or say 'we can help with this' / 'I can build this for you'

assertions.name: no_em_dashes
assertions.description: Contains no em dashes

assertions.name: no_emojis
assertions.description: Contains no emoji characters

assertions.name: label_sharper_than_disclosure
assertions.description: Label reframes or sharpens what Priya said — not just repeating her own words back verbatim

evals.name: scenario-b-peer-agency-owner
evals.prompt:
This prospect looks like a peer — another AI/automation consultant. What do I send back?

Conversation:
[Me, Day 0 connection note]: Hey Steven, came across Rivet AI while exploring the automation space. The founder-led stage you're building through is one I find genuinely interesting to follow. Looking forward to being in your network.

[Steven, Day 1]: Appreciate it Aleem. Checked out your profile — looks like we're in overlapping territory. Always good to know other builders in the space. What are you focused on lately?

Profile: Steven Roberts, Founder of Rivet AI (solo consultancy, AI strategy and implementation for mid-market companies, based in Denver).
evals.expected_output: A Scenario B output with peer-track framing. Key signal: Steven is an AI consultant himself — not a direct client, potentially a referral partner. Aleem should NOT pitch services. Should flag the peer dynamic in the meta-line's Tactic. Should ask a calibrated question that surfaces whether there's a partnership/overflow/subcontract fit ('do you handle all the build side yourself or bring in specialists'). Meta-line phase is Qualify (peer track).

assertions.name: under_1000_chars
assertions.description: Message body (excluding meta-line) is under 1000 characters

assertions.name: contains_voss_label
assertions.description: Contains at least one Voss label

assertions.name: no_sales_pitch
assertions.description: Does not pitch NexusPoint services as if Steven is a prospective client

assertions.name: no_call_ask_for_sale
assertions.description: Does not ask Steven to 'hop on a call' as a prospective buyer

assertions.name: partnership_or_peer_framing
assertions.description: Frames the conversation as peer-to-peer / partnership territory — e.g., asks about his build-side setup, overflow work, specialist partners, or referral dynamics

assertions.name: meta_flags_peer_track
assertions.description: Meta-line (Phase or Tactic) flags that this is a peer/referral-partner track, not a direct-client track

assertions.name: includes_meta_line
assertions.description: Output includes Phase: and Tactic: meta-line

assertions.name: no_em_dashes
assertions.description: Contains no em dashes

assertions.name: no_emojis
assertions.description: Contains no emoji characters

assertions.name: calibrated_question_present
assertions.description: Contains a calibrated question that surfaces the nature of his build-side setup
